response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering expertise with 14+ years of experience across diverse domains, this principal-level role requires deep specialization in hardware architecture, SoC/IP modeling, and AI accelerator optimization—areas with no evidence in the resume. The performance optimization background (handling distributed web traffic) differs fundamentally from the hardware-level SoC and GPU optimization work this role demands.
strengths: Exceeds educational and experience requirements with 14+ years of demonstrated software engineering leadership and multiple staff-level positions
Proven performance optimization skills: designed low-latency systems handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O, caching, and fiber-based architectures
Architectural thinking and ability to work across technology stacks (D, C++, TypeScript, Java) and domains (web, embedded, GIS, infrastructure)
concerns: Zero demonstrated expertise in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD instruction sets, or AI accelerator optimization—core requirements for the role
No background in deep neural network training/inference optimization, SoC/IP modeling, or hardware-software co-design
Career trajectory is entirely software-focused (web, backend, mobile); lacks the hardware architecture foundation needed for a principal-level performance architect role

key_factors.factor: Hardware architecture and AI accelerator expertise gap
Exceeds minimum years of experience and educational requirements
Performance optimization background not in target domain (software vs. hardware)
